messages.subject: [R-sig-teaching] R example applications
messages.body_plain:
I am going to be teaching an R course for beginners (for geoscience professionals)  and I would like to start with some really interesting  and visual applications to get their attention and show them the power of R before getting down to the some R basics. Any good ideas?

Mark Cave


Dr Mark Cave
Medical Geology
Land, Soil and Coast
British Geological Survey
Keyworth
Nottingham
NG12 5GG
UK
E-mail: mrca at bgs.ac.uk<mailto:mrca at bgs.ac.uk>
Tel direct: 0115 9363526
Tel switchboard: 0115 9363100




  ________________________________

messages.subject: [R-sig-teaching] R example applications
messages.body_plain:
The package misc3d gives an example of a 3D representation of a volcano, then it includes it as a 
dynamic graph in a pdf file. "Dynamic graph" means that you can "turn" the graphe in the pdf and 
change the point of view. It exists for several years now, but since nobody knows it, it is still  
quite impressive.

Christophe

messages.subject: [R-sig-teaching] R example applications
messages.body_plain:
Hi Mark, Charlotte Wickham recently built a course with us on Working with
Geospatial Data in R. It contains tons of cool examples/visualizations that
may appeal to this crowd. Happy to get you and your students set up with a
free subscription if helpful.

She's also created a data (R) package containing all of the datasets used
in the course: https://github.com/cwickham/geospatial

Cheers,
Nick

-- 
Nick Carchedi
Director of Course Development | DataCamp

messages.subject: [R-sig-teaching] Waiting to confirm page change...
messages.body_plain:
Dear All:

I am trying to create a histogram for the variable "sr" in the R data
set LifeCycleSavings$sr. But after I submit the hist code, it took long
time. I had to stop R session. It says "Waiting to confirm page change...".

I have no idea what is this about?

any ideas will help.

with thanks
abou
_____________________
AbouEl-Makarim Aboueissa, PhD
University of Southern Maine
Department of Mathematics and Statistics

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-teaching] Waiting to confirm page change...
messages.body_plain:
This doesn't have anything to do with how to use R to teach statistics, so
it should go to the main r-help mailing list, not the special interest
group for teaching.  In addition, it will be hard for people to diagnose
without a reproducible example.  When you email the main list, include code
that will make this happen reliably so people can work with it and figure
out what the problem is.

messages.subject: [R-sig-teaching] Waiting to confirm page change...
messages.body_plain:
Dear Jeff:

Thank you very much.

Could you please let me know what kind of questions should someone uses R
to teach statistics ask and post in the r-sig-teaching list? This would be
very helpful in the future.

I am using this list all the times long time ago.

Once again, thank you very much for your help.

with thanks
abou

messages.subject: [R-sig-teaching] Waiting to confirm page change...
messages.body_plain:
Dear Abou

questions like yours are suitable for the R-help list.  For this list,
questions about teaching, like "what experiences have people had teaching
with the mosaic package"?  or a recent one asking for suggestions about
teaching geoscientists to use R, good datasets and examples to use, etc.

regards,

albyn

messages.subject: [R-sig-teaching] Waiting to confirm page change...
messages.body_plain:
It means you have inadvertently told R to check with you before clearing the previous graph.  See
?devAskNewPage

Click the graph window or enter return in the console.

Sent from my iPhone
